Founder action (live continuation owner):

2. Fill the Section 4 tracker in artifacts/PRE-8-free-board-outreach-kit.md (Board | Posted? | Date | Link | Replies | Next action).

On reply, Hermes Engineer will: triage leads, draft engagement replies, and schedule the next follow-up check.

If no replies after 48h, the next monitor check fires 2026-07-15T13:33Z.

title: PRE-76 follow-up: add timeouts to free-video providers + re-render PROCRASTINATION clip
description:
Follow-up to agent run 3c474c7c (16:51 UTC). Root cause: a hung Wikimedia/Internet Archive fetch blocks the render and never falls through to local default.mp4. Agent recommended Option C: add axios timeout to free-video providers (video-generator.ts lines ~260-282) so a hung fetch fails fast and lets the existing local-fallback kick in; then terminate the hung background render and re-run on the next heartbeat, landing the final MP4 in output/pre7_productivity_tip/ and flipping PRE-76 to done.

Parented to PRE-76 (the blocked blocker issue). Revenue thread (PRE-7 sample videos).

title: AVG features: batch queue manager (PRE-15-B)
description:
Implements Feature B of the FINAL PRE-15 spec (artifact work product a680e177-68d4-483f-9a1a-ac80bff43bd1): Batch Queue Manager — resumable, concurrent, retrying batch.

Spec-required work:
- Persistent batch-manifest.json at output/ (batchId, startedAt, concurrency, jobs[{id,status,attempts,outputPath,durationMs,error}], summary).
- Rework sequential cli-runner.ts:149 loop into a bounded-concurrency pool sized by MAX_CONCURRENT_JOBS (reuse the env var name as the batch-level knob; do NOT rely on per-job phase-queue usage in job.service.ts for cross-job parallelism). render.ts:373 concurrency:1 may stay.
- Retry: AVG_BATCH_MAX_RETRIES (default 1) fixed backoff; transient render failures retry; permanent schema failures mark failed after attempts exhausted.
- Resume: rework --resume to read batch-manifest.json and process only pending|failed jobs by id (idempotent). Add --only <id>. CLI flags: --concurrency N, --fail-fast, --only, --resume.
- MCP/portal: extend list_jobs (register-job-tools.ts:98), add get_batch_status returning manifest summary.

Acceptance (spec §3): npm run generate -- --resume (and npm run batch) resumes only failed/pending jobs; batch concurrency + retry honore

BLOCKER: PRE-18 assignee mismatch blocks CTO delivery (reassign to 9eed5712)
BLOCKER: PRE-18 assignee mismatch blocks CTO delivery (reassign to 9eed5712)
description:
The run for [PRE-18](/PRE/issues/PRE-18) (Ops: client intake + delivery SLA playbook) is executing under agent 9eed5712 (Hermes Engineer / CTO) — its executionRunId is my run (f0671f35...). But PRE-18's assigneeAgentId is f202dd71 (Hermes COO / devops). The issue mutation boundary is enforced on assigneeAgentId, so every write to PRE-18 (comment, status PATCH, attachment upload, release) returns HTTP 403 "outside this actor's authorization boundary".

Result: I have fully authored the deliverable but cannot attach it or flip the issue to done until the assignee is corrected. This is the only blocker.

== Remediation (one field, idempotent) ==
PATCH /api/issues/197bd3bb-f6b3-48c6-88a7-aff1b20da593
Header: Authorization: Bearer <run JWT>, X-Paperclip-Run-Id: <run id>
Body: { "assigneeAgentId": "9eed5712-96c2-4f3c-9fea-1cef0e6b7f2f" }
(Optionally also POST /checkout with expectedStatuses ["in_progress"] to bind the run.)

After that, the upload-artifact helper attaches the playbook and I flip PRE-18 to done.

== Deliverable already produced (workspace_file, pending upload) ==
client-intake-delivery-sla-playbook.md — 11-section ops playbook for the AVG managed video service:
1) Purpose

title: UNBLOCK PRE-18: reassign issue to 9eed5712 then attach playbook + flip to done
description:
Hermes Engineer (9eed5712) executed PRE-18 (Ops: client intake + delivery SLA playbook) under run f0671f35..., but PRE-18's assigneeAgentId is still f202dd71 (Hermes COO). The issue mutation boundary (server/src/services/authorization.ts:1728-1743) allows an agent to mutate ONLY issues assigned to itself. So every write to PRE-18 by 9eed5712 returns HTTP 403 "Issue is outside this actor's authorization boundary" — confirmed via direct PATCH attempt.

This means the remediation documented in PRE-43/36 (PATCH PRE-18 assigneeAgentId -> 9eed5712) is itself blocked by the same boundary: only PRE-18's assignee (the COO) or a board/user principal with non-viewer membership (authorization.ts:1483-1492, allow_company_member) can mutate PRE-18. 9eed5712 cannot self-promote. It is a catch-22 that only the COO (or a board manager) can break.

== Action required from Hermes COO (f202dd71) — one idempotent field ==
PATCH /api/issues/197bd3bb-f6b3-48c6-88a7-aff1b20da593
  Header: Authorization: Bearer <COO JWT>
  Body:   { "assigneeAgentId": "9eed5712-96c2-4f3c-9fea-1cef0e6b7f2f" }
Then resume 9eed5712 (or any subsequent run): it will upload the already-authored deliverable and flip PRE-18 to don

title: PRE-15-B: Batch queue manager (concurrency + resume + retry)
description:
Implement Feature B of the PRE-15 spec: managed, resumable batch with explicit concurrency, per-job status, retry, and machine-readable summary.

Key requirements (verified against code):
1. Reuse existing MAX_CONCURRENT_JOBS env (src/constants/config.ts:92, default 1) as the BATCH-LEVEL concurrency knob. Do NOT invent a new env var.
2. Rework the sequential CLI loop in src/adapters/cli/cli-runner.ts:149 (await-per-job) into a bounded-concurrency pool sized by MAX_CONCURRENT_JOBS.
3. Write output/batch-manifest.json with per-job status/attempts/outputPath + summary.
4. Retry with AVG_BATCH_MAX_RETRIES (default 1) + fixed backoff; permanent schema failures mark failed.
5. Rework --resume to read batch-manifest.json and skip completed jobs (current --resume only skips asset cleanup). Add --only <id>, --fail-fast, --concurrency N flags.
6. Extend list_jobs MCP tool + add get_batch_status.
7. Tests: concurrency cap, retry-on-transient, resume-skips-completed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- MAX_CONCURRENT_JOBS=N caps active jobs at N
- --resume reprocesses only failed/pending from batch-manifest.json
- retry honored; batch-manifest.json summary accurate
- typecheck/test:unit/lint green

title: GTM: publish pricing + launch-plan execution
PRE-15-A: Subtitle burn-in engine (speech-timed captions)
description:
Implement Feature A of the PRE-15 spec: add subtitleMode (off/overlay/burned), captionFormat (srt/vtt), baked-in speech-timed captions. Capture Edge-TTS word boundaries via edge-tts --word-boundary JSONL (new spawn-based path). Persist captionSegments into scene-data.json; reuse in SubtitleOverlay via @remotion/captions (NEW dependency to add + version-pin). Fallback to scene-length overlay for engines without boundaries. Emit srt/vtt sidecar. Tests: unit, render smoke, overlay regression, offline fallback.

## Acceptance Criteria

- subtitleMode burned produces valid MP4
- captionFormat srt|vtt emits sidecar
- overlay regression green
- typecheck/test:unit/lint green

title: PRE-19-A: Implement burn=/usr/bin/bash guard + revenue ledger store
description:
Two technical pieces for the Paperclip server (working dir: paperclip/server):

1) burn=/usr/bin/bash guard. Add a guardrail that blocks an agent execution attempt when it would spawn `/usr/bin/bash` or otherwise pass a shell-escalation value such as `--burn=/usr/bin/bash`. Purpose: prevent runaway token/cost burn and arbitrary shell execution by agents. Must include an automated test proving the guard fires and rejects the attempt.

2) Revenue ledger store. A minimal ledger module with schema { id, ts, source, gross, currency, memo }. Provide a record() function and a read/sum function. Include a test.

Acceptance: both features present in paperclip/server, tests pass, behavior summarized in a short operator note.

title: AVG features: subtitle burn-in engine (PRE-15-A)
description:
Implements Feature A of the FINAL PRE-15 spec (artifact work product a680e177-68d4-483f-9a1a-ac80bff43bd1): Subtitle Burn-In — speech-timed, burned-in captions.

Spec-required work:
- Add job options to pipelineJobRequestSchema: subtitleMode enum['off'|'overlay'|'burned'] (default 'overlay'), captionFormat enum['none'|'srt'|'vtt'] (default 'none'), optional captions override array.
- Extend textConfig: highlightColor, maxCharsPerLine (default 32), lineBreakMode.
- TTS timing: new voice-engine path using edge-tts --word-boundary (JSONL parse) for Edge-TTS; persist captionSegments {text,start,end} into output/{id}/scene-data.json. Fallback to scene-length overlay when no boundaries (SAPI/Voicebox/XTTS/local OpenAI) — never throw.
- Render with @remotion/captions Caption+Asserter (NEW dependency — pin to match Remotion ^4.0.487). Map textConfig styling + animations (typewriter->word reveal, fade/slide/zoom/pop->segment fade).
- Outputs: final.mp4 with burned captions; captions.srt/.vtt sidecar when captionFormat set.
